site.title: fstache
site.tagline: The Future of Static Site Generation
site.description: Blazing fast mustache compiling with premium aesthetics and developer-first workflows.

marketing_banner_html: LAUNCH EVENT fstache v2.0 Playgrounds are now live! Explore the compiler sandbox below. Try simulator →

hero.subheading: Compile complex mustache layouts in microseconds, zero overhead, pure performance.

features.title: Microsecond Execution
features.description: Engineered with maximum efficiency. Mustache templates render with minimal memory footprint and zero CPU waste.

features.title: Modular Partials
features.description: Effortlessly compose complex layouts. Nest headers, footers, card grids, and custom wrappers with ease.

features.title: Aesthetic-First Design
features.description: Includes fully responsive CSS custom properties, glassmorphism containers, and beautiful gradients out of the box.

testimonials.quote: Compiling our product catalog documentation went from 8 seconds to 12 milliseconds. fstache is an absolute game-changer for static site generation.
testimonials.author: Sarah Chen

testimonials.quote: We integrated fstache into our edge workers. It renders heavily-nested static dashboards with zero latency spikes and under 2ms TTFB.
testimonials.author: Marcus Vance

blog_posts.title: Unlocking Microsecond Render Times in Static Site Generators
blog_posts.teaser: How we re-engineered string concatenation tokenizers to bypass standard engine bottlenecks, achieving rendering under 1.5ms for production dashboards.
blog_posts.author: Sarah Chen

blog_posts.title: Designing Sleek Glassmorphism UIs with Tailwind CSS v4
blog_posts.teaser: A comprehensive deep dive into new utility variables, backdrop-filters, and gradient borders in Tailwind v4 that compose modern dashboard environments.
blog_posts.author: David Lin

blog_posts.title: The Power of Delimiter Switching in Complex Markup
blog_posts.teaser: Mustache delimiter switching enables literal syntax blocks for other templates (like Vue, Angular, or Django templates) without escape sequence issues.
blog_posts.author: Elena Rostova

blog_posts.title: Edge Worker Renders: Bypassing Cold Start Latencies
blog_posts.teaser: Edge architectures require light, zero-dependency HTML pipelines. We demonstrate how eliminating bundle dependencies enables 2ms TTFB across regions.
blog_posts.author: Marcus Vance

blog_posts.title: How CDNify Scaled Dashboards to 10M Monthly Readers
blog_posts.teaser: A technical case study on how CDNify transitioned their analytics status panel to pre-rendered Mustache layouts, slashing server bills by 85%.
blog_posts.author: Jessica Miller

blog_posts.title: Standardizing JSON Schema Layout Contracts
blog_posts.teaser: Enforcing schema boundaries between data services and template presentation layers ensures safety, code cleanliness, and rapid UI iteration.
blog_posts.author: Alex Mercer

blog_posts.title: Mitigating Template Injection Vulnerabilities in Static Sites
blog_posts.teaser: Template injection occurs when untrusted user inputs are dynamically compiled. We analyze safety controls, sandboxed build nodes, and HTML escaping protocols in Mustache.
blog_posts.author: Liam Vance

blog_posts.title: Deploying Dynamic Static Pre-Renders to Cloudflare Workers
blog_posts.teaser: By combining CDN edge logic with pre-compiled layout nodes, developers can achieve static generation speed with dynamic personalization parameters on the fly.
blog_posts.author: Sophia Loren

additions: Added standard support for recursive partial templates.
Integrated Tailwind CSS v4 support in layouts.
fixes: Fixed context stack bubbling lookup on empty child collections.
Resolved whitespace stripping tags issue in nested delimiter shifts.

additions: Introduced layout slot parent template composition.
fixes: Resolved SVG rendering crash on Safari 16.4 browsers.
Fixed HTML character escaping logic on ampersand tags.

faqs.question: Why is Mustache called 'logic-less' templating?
faqs.answer: Because it doesn't contain explicit conditional logic statements (like 'if/else' or loops). The rendering behavior is driven entirely by the structure of the data you pass in. This keeps templates highly readable, maintainable, and extremely performant.

faqs.question: What is the benefit of using unescaped HTML tags?
faqs.answer: By default, variables are HTML-escaped to prevent XSS attacks. Using triple braces allows you to inject raw, styled HTML snippets directly into the layout from trusted CMS sources.

faqs.question: Can I nest partial templates dynamically?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mustache supports nesting partial templates. Partials are loaded at compile time, which enables complex layout composition while maintaining maximum performance.

faqs.question: What is context stack bubbling and how is it resolved?
faqs.answer: Mustache searches parent scopes if a variable is not found in the current object context. To prevent infinite loops during recursion, always specify empty termination properties (like 'children: []') on child records.

faqs.question: How does dynamic delimiter switching work?
faqs.answer: Using the syntax '{{=<% %>=}}', you can temporarily redefine Mustache tags to '<%' and '%>'. This is highly useful when compiling content that naturally uses double curlies, like AngularJS or Vue markup.

faqs.question: Is there a production-ready CLI compiler for pipelines?
faqs.answer: Yes. fstache compiles using Python's UV runner for sandbox Chevron builds, or can be packaged inside Docker containers to run standard Mustache compilers in CI/CD pipelines.

faqs.question: Can I style compiled HTML templates with Tailwind CSS?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. By referencing a browser compilation script or incorporating the Tailwind compiler, you can style all templates using standard utility classes and responsive grids.

faqs.question: How does the implicit iterator work?
faqs.answer: When looping over plain arrays of strings or numbers, the implicit iterator '{{.}}' acts as a placeholder representing the current array element, keeping your JSON files flat and lightweight.

faqs.question: Can I use Mustache templates for email generation?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Because Mustache outputs static HTML and does not require JavaScript engines or runtime dependencies, it is the industry standard for compiling transactional HTML email templates with embedded inline CSS.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Mustache and Handlebars?
faqs.answer: Handlebars is a superset of Mustache. While Mustache enforces a strict logic-less standard without helpers or custom blocks, Handlebars extends the syntax to support custom registerHelper blocks, execution logic, and dynamic paths.

faqs.question: Does fstache support server-side rendering (SSR)?
faqs.answer: Yes. Due to the high execution speed (under 1.5ms per render), fstache can be deployed inside Node.js, Go, or Python web servers to process server-side rendering for millions of requests with minimal memory overhead.

faqs.question: How do I secure Mustache template injection paths?
faqs.answer: Template injection occurs when untrusted user input is directly evaluated as template source code. Always separate static layout structure from runtime data variables by pre-defining all compiled layouts and validating variables against JSON schemas before render execution.
